thesis_claim: Living well with schizophrenia is governed less by the illness itself than by things the word 'support' usually ignores, a body that dies about fifteen years early of preventable heart disease, a world that treats the victim as the threat, and a recovery rate real enough to matter and contested enough that no single number is honest.
the_risk: State the mortality gap and violence-victimisation inversion plainly; refuse to resolve the recovery-rate contest with the comforting number, give the real spread and why it is disputed.
recovery_language: PERMITTED and REQUIRED here (CHRONIC; NEURODIVERGENT-only ban does not apply).

block_plan.quote_verbatim: I stepped off that vehicle later in West London with a radically different and, as far as I am concerned, 'corrected' brain from the one I had when I stepped on it.
block_plan.attribution: Chadwick PK, peer-professional first-person account, Schizophrenia Bulletin 2007
block_plan.fallback: If verbatim quote cannot be confirmed against PMC full text, render SCENE as third-person body copy citing Chadwick 2007 (or Gardiner 2021), block does not ship without a resolved citation.

block_plan.content: THE HARDEST RISK. Long-term outcome is heterogeneous; recovery is real but CONTESTED, 13.5% by a strict combined clinical+social criterion in chronic samples (Jaaskelainen), 38% recovery / 58% remission in first-episode cohorts (Lally). Give the range and WHY it differs (sample + definition); do NOT pick the comforting number.

stats: ~14.5 years of potential life lost; life expectancy ~64.7 (Hjorthoj)
cardiovascular disease is the leading cause of death (HR 1.85; accidents kill >2x as many as suicide), the gap is preventable physical illness, not suicide alone
people with severe mental illness commit ~1 in 20 violent crimes (~5% population-attributable)
victimisation (20-34%) far exceeds perpetration (2-13%), the victim, not the threat

block_plan.content: The gap between capacity and employment, and what supported employment (IPS) shows: more than doubles competitive employment (RR 2.40; 55% vs 28% working at least a day).
Housing, income, social network, outcome tracks these as hard as it tracks symptoms (Kirkbride; framing, qualitative).

steps: What a family member can actually do: learn early-warning signs
Recognise carer burden (~38% severe in one sample; rises with symptom severity and illness duration) and seek carer support as a real thing

block_plan.content: One onward path (back to the hub / a support tool).
